text: What is the LOQ of your method?
help_text: Enter the Limit of Quantification. Lower is better. Scoring: <1 = Excellent, <3 = Good, <10 = Acceptable, >10 = Poor.

text: How many peaks have Rs < 1.5 (including admixtures)?
help_text: Enter the number of peaks with resolution below 1.5. Scoring: 0 = Excellent, 1 = Good, 2 = Acceptable, >3 = Poor.

text: What is the Precision of your method (RSD%)?
help_text: Enter the RSD%. Scoring: <2.5% = Excellent, <3.5% = Good, <5% = Acceptable, >5% = Poor.

text: What is the Repeatability of your method (RSD%)?
help_text: Enter the RSD%. Scoring: <2% = Excellent, <3% = Good, <5% = Acceptable, >5% = Poor.

text: What is the Accuracy of your method (% error in standard solution)?
help_text: Enter the % error. Scoring: <1.5% = Excellent, <2% = Good, <3.5% = Acceptable, >3.5% = Poor.

text: What is the Recovery of your assay (% error)?
help_text: Enter the % error. Scoring: <3% = Excellent, <4% = Good, <5% = Acceptable, >5% = Poor.

text: What is the Linearity (r²) of your method?
help_text: Enter the r² value (e.g. 0.9991). Scoring: ≥0.999 = Excellent, ≥0.998 = Good, ≥0.997 = Acceptable, <0.997 = Poor.

text: System Suitability: What is the retention factor (k) of the first analyte?
help_text: Enter the k value. Scoring: 2-3 = Excellent, 3-6 = Good, 6-10 or 1.5-2 = Acceptable, outside 1-10 = Poor.

text: System Suitability: What is the Tailing Factor (Tf)?
help_text: Enter the Tf value. Scoring: 0.8-1.2 = Excellent, 0.7-1.5 = Good, 0.6-2 = Acceptable, outside = Poor.

text: System Suitability: How many theoretical plates (N) does your column have?
help_text: Enter the N value. Scoring: >4000 = Excellent, >2000 = Good, >1000 = Acceptable, <1000 = Poor.

text: Has carry-over been checked?
help_text: Indicate whether carry-over has been evaluated for your method.

text: Robustness: How many parameters were tested minus how many were non-robust?
help_text: Enter the result of (parameters tested - non-robust parameters). Scoring: >5 = Excellent, >4 = Good, >3 = Acceptable, ≤2 = Poor.

text: How many analytes does your method cover?
help_text: Enter the number of analytes. Scoring: >4 = Excellent, 3 = Good, 2 = Acceptable, 1 = Poor.

text: How many samples can be simultaneously pretreated? (Enter 11 if no pretreatment is needed)
help_text: Enter the number of simultaneous samples. Scoring: >10 = Excellent, >6 = Good, >2 = Acceptable, 1 = Poor.

text: What is the total analysis time including recalibration in gradient elution (minutes)?
help_text: Enter the time in minutes. Scoring: <5 = Excellent, <10 = Good, <20 = Acceptable, >20 = Poor.

text: What is the pretreatment time (minutes)?
help_text: Enter the pretreatment time. Scoring: <15 = Excellent, <30 = Good, <45 = Acceptable, >45 = Poor.

text: What detector does your method use?
options: UV-UV/VIS;PDA or FLD;Specific Detectors (ELSD/CAD etc);MS or MS/MS
help_text: Select the detector type. Scoring: UV = Excellent (cheapest), MS/MS = Poor (most expensive).
params: {"UV-UV/VIS": 100, "PDA or FLD": 75, "Specific Detectors (ELSD/CAD etc)": 50, "MS or MS/MS": 25}

text: What is the cost of the column used (EUR)?
help_text: Enter the column cost. Scoring: <500 = Excellent, <1000 = Good, <1500 = Acceptable, >1500 = Poor.

text: What is the mobile phase consumption per run (mL)?
help_text: Enter the total mL of mobile phase per run. Scoring: <5mL = Excellent, <10mL = Good, <20mL = Acceptable, >20mL = Poor.

text: What is the cost of reagents used including derivatization per 100 samples (EUR)?
help_text: Enter the total reagent cost. Scoring: 0 = Excellent, low cost = Good, medium = Acceptable, high = Poor.

text: What is the volume of pretreatment extraction solvent used per sample (mL)?
help_text: Enter the total mL. Scoring: <3mL = Excellent, <10mL = Good, <20mL = Acceptable, >20mL = Poor.

text: Is plastic used in the pretreatment procedure?
help_text: Select the level of plastic use in your pretreatment procedure.

text: What is the Hazard score for your method? (NFPA score × mL for both analysis and pretreatment)
help_text: Multiply the NFPA hazard score by the total mL used. Scoring: <5 = Excellent, <15 = Good, <25 = Acceptable, >25 = Poor.
